scenario: Stargate Healthcare evaluates Camunda 8.8 client choices for a new worker codebase that 12 developers will own. The architect is checking which language client is positioned in Writing good workers as a worked example for Java teams building job workers, alongside roughly 800 jobs per day.
question: Which client does the Writing good workers page position as the example for Java teams writing Camunda 8.8 workers?

options.text: The Camunda Java Client linked from the get-started repository is positioned as the example for Java teams writing worker code against Camunda 8.8 in the Writing good workers best practice page
The Camunda Spring Boot Starter is positioned as the only recommended way to write workers in Java, and the standalone Camunda Java Client is shown only as a counter-example to avoid in new projects
A vendor-neutral Kafka-based client is positioned as the example for Java workers; direct gRPC clients to Zeebe are described as legacy patterns to phase out during the migration to 8.8
The Camunda CLI is positioned as the example for Java workers; the Writing good workers page recommends shell-script-driven invocations of the CLI for simple Java worker implementations in 8.8

a.text: Correct. The Java section of Writing good workers points Java teams at the official Camunda Java Client in the camunda-platform-get-started Java directory and uses it to show how worker code looks in Java for Camunda 8.8.

b.text: Incorrect. The Spring Boot Starter is one option, but the Writing good workers page directly points to the standalone Java Client for its example without treating it as a counter-example.

c.text: Incorrect. The page does not suggest a Kafka-based client or describe direct gRPC clients as legacy in 8.8.

d.text: Incorrect. The CLI is not positioned as the example for Java workers in Writing good workers.

explanation: For Java workers, the Camunda 8.8 Writing good workers page uses the official Camunda Java Client from the camunda-platform-get-started repository as the example implementation.
